title: UAP Sensor Manifest
description: Machine-readable per-sensor specification standard for UAP detection hardware. Organizes sensors by modality and captures, per sensor, the structured spec + provenance metadata DisclosureOS's SensorReading lacks (sensor type, timing precision, measurement units + uncertainty, raw-data format, calibration). Any operator — vendor, research group, or institution — publishes its own manifest instance against this schema so datasets from different sensor arrays can be compared directly. Extends disclosurefoundation/disclosureos.

orgUrl.description: Optional URL for the publishing organization.

generatedAt.description: ISO date the manifest was generated.

futureUpgrades.description: Planned hardware additions declared ahead of deployment, so data consumers can anticipate new streams. Entries here are NOT part of the live sensor array.

link.description: Public product or reference page, where the operator chooses to share one.

manufacturerModel.description: Hardware class descriptor. Operators MAY withhold specific make/model identities and publish spec-level class descriptors instead (e.g. 'Dual GM-tube counter' rather than a product name); the specs object carries the comparable technical detail either way.

inHouse.description: True if operator-built rather than OEM.

compute.description: The compute unit this sensor is attached to. A platform is not one monolithic node — it is several compute units, and each sensor is tied to one. The ELDÆON Dionysus platform has four (PC, Jetson, Pi, Pi 5); this field names whichever the sensor is wired to. Also appears as the leading '{compute-id}' segment of rawData.locatorPattern.

disclosureosMapping.description: P1 — how this sensor maps to DisclosureOS SensorReading. proposedSensorType flags values not yet in their closed enum.

proposedSensorType.description: True = this value does not exist in DisclosureOS's current enum and is proposed by ELDÆON (P1).

timing.description: P2 — timing provenance, absent from DisclosureOS (bare timestamp string).

rawData.description: P4 — raw-data format + locator, vs DisclosureOS's rawDataAvailable boolean.

format.description: e.g. NDJSON.gz, WAV, MJPEG, H264, FITS, CSV.

locatorPattern.description: S3 key pattern or path convention. Leading '{compute-id}' segment is the compute unit the sensor is tied to (see the sensor-level 'compute' field).

measurements.description: P3 — measured quantities with units + GUM uncertainty. Uncertainty fields nullable until a budget exists.

unit.description: UCUM-style unit string, e.g. dB[mW], uT, ppm, /min.

calibration.description: P6 — calibration provenance. Replaces DisclosureOS's calibrated:boolean. Deliberately separates what an operator ACTUALLY does today (currentMethod) from the traceable reference it has identified (traceableReference + referenceInUse). Honest nulls are first-class: declaring 'no calibration' is itself information a data consumer needs, and makes calibration gaps visible and comparable across operators.

currentMethod.description: What the operator actually does today (e.g. 'factory calibration file on onboard SD card', 'oriented to true north at install', '395 nm UV source response check'). Null = no calibration currently performed.

traceableReference.description: Traceable reference — in use or identified as a candidate. e.g. ADS-B Exchange, USGS/INTERMAGNET, GPS/UTC, NIST SP 800-22.

referenceInUse.description: True = traceableReference is part of current practice. False = identified candidate only, not yet used. Null = undetermined.

status.description: none = no calibration; candidate-identified = a reference is named but not used; in-practice = a real method is performed but not fully budgeted; documented = method + uncertainty budget published.

notes.description: Open questions or method caveats (e.g. 'field-deployable thermal reference under evaluation').

specs.description: Free-form hardware specifications from the operator's spec sheet (resolution, frame rate, FOV, range, sensitivity, dynamic range, power, interface, etc.). Open object — keys vary by modality.
